GOLDFIELD, New, Dec. 10
Will the bull pen" tactics which
worked so much disaster in the
Occur d'Alene strike years ago be
repeated in Goldfield? This is
what interested observers of the
military occupation OF Nevada's Ell
Dorado are asking


With eight companies of Uncle
Sans regulars encamped near the
mines and rapid fire guns mounted
here and there, it looks as though
desperate measures were contem-
plated, despite the fact that, so far
there has been less cause for ex-
treme offensive precautions than
in most strikes. Is the ounce of
prevention" which the troops are
supposed to represent developing
into capital operation" people
inquire.


GOLDFIELD, Dec. 16.-From
Sheriff w. A. ingalls, and a delega-
ton from the American Federation
of Labor, Funston has obtained sui
ficient information to convince him
that the presence of military
force in this city is imperative. AL
though Funston has heard hun
dreds while investigating the situa-
tion here, nothing has impressed
him as much as the statements of
ingalls, and the American Fedora
ton men. ingalls, admitted he was
powerless in case trouble started.


The federal commissioners, Sec.
Lawrence o. Murray, OF the depart
